# @embra/i18n agent guide

Quick reference for AI coding agents. Use README.md for full docs.

Install:

```sh
npm add @embra/reactivity @embra/i18n
```

## Core

```ts
import { I18n, detectLang, type Locales } from "@embra/i18n";

const locales: Locales = {
  en: {
    stock: { fruit: "apple" },
    eat: "{{name}} eats {{fruit}}.",
    apple: "{{@}} apples",
    "apple@0": "No apple",
    "apple@1": "An apple",
  },
};

const i18n = new I18n("en", locales);
i18n.t("stock.fruit"); // "apple"
i18n.t("eat", { fruit: "apple", name: "CRIMX" }); // "CRIMX eats apple."
i18n.t("apple", { "@": 1 }); // "An apple"
i18n.t("apple", { "@": 3 }); // "3 apples"
detectLang(["en", "zh-CN"]);
```

- Translation keys can be nested, for example `stock.fruit`.
- Template placeholders use `{{name}}`.
- Plural/count matching uses modifier keys such as `apple@0`, `apple@1`; pass the value as `{ "@": count }`.
- Use `I18n.preload(lang, loader)` for dynamic locale imports.

## React

```tsx
import { I18n } from "@embra/i18n";
import { I18nProvider, Trans, useTranslate } from "@embra/i18n/react";

function Message() {
  const t = useTranslate();
  return (
    <Trans message={t("eat")}>
      <strong data-t-slot="name">{t("author")}</strong>
      <i data-t-slot="fruit">{t("fruit")}</i>
    </Trans>
  );
}

<I18nProvider i18n={new I18n("en", locales)}>
  <Message />
</I18nProvider>;
```

- React APIs come from `@embra/i18n/react`.
- `useTranslate()` subscribes to language and locale changes.
- React `<Trans>` uses `data-t-slot="name"` to match `{{name}}`.
- For one placeholder, React `<Trans>` can use its child without `data-t-slot`.

## Astro

```astro
---
import { I18n } from "@embra/i18n";
import { Trans } from "@embra/i18n/astro";

const i18n = new I18n("en", locales);
const t = i18n.t;
---

<Trans message={t("eat")}>
  <strong slot="name">{t("author")}</strong>
  <i slot="fruit">{t("fruit")}</i>
</Trans>
```

- Astro APIs come from `@embra/i18n/astro`; prefer `import { Trans } from "@embra/i18n/astro"`.
- Astro `<Trans>` uses Astro named slots like `slot="name"` to match `{{name}}`.
- For one placeholder, Astro `<Trans>` can use the default slot.
- Astro `<Trans>` renders at build time or on the server. Use a framework island for client-side language switching.

## Canonical imports and slots

| Context | Import | Trans slot syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Core | `@embra/i18n` | `{{name}}` values |
| React | `@embra/i18n/react` | `data-t-slot="name"` |
| Astro | `@embra/i18n/astro` | `slot="name"` |

Missing Trans slots are rendered back as `{{name}}`.
